Protein names: Ergothioneine biosynthesis protein A [Includes: L-histidine N(alpha)-methyltransferase (EC 2.1.1.44); Hercynylcysteine S-oxide synthase (EC 1.21.3.10)]
Organism: Aspergillus fumigatus (strain ATCC MYA-4609 / CBS 101355 / FGSC A1100 / Af293) (Neosartorya fumigata)
Protein families: Methyltransferase superfamily, EgtD family; EgtB family
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: In the N-terminal section; belongs to the methyltransferase superfamily. EgtD family. {ECO:0000305}.; SIMILARITY: In the C-terminal section; belongs to the EgtB family. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=L-histidine + 3 S-adenosyl-L-methionine = hercynine + 3 S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + 3 H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:38471, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:15781, ChEBI:CHEBI:57595, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789; EC=2.1.1.44; Evidence={ECO:0000250|UniProtKB:Q7RX33}; PhysiologicalDirection=left-to-right; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:38472; Evidence={ECO:0000250|UniProtKB:Q7RX33}; CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=hercynine + L-cysteine + O2 = S-(hercyn-2-yl)-L-cysteine S-oxide + H2O; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:42704, ChEBI:CHEBI:15377, ChEBI:CHEBI:15379, ChEBI:CHEBI:15781, ChEBI:CHEBI:35235, ChEBI:CHEBI:82706; EC=1.21.3.10; Evidence={ECO:0000250|UniProtKB:Q7RX33}; PhysiologicalDirection=left-to-right; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:42705; Evidence={ECO:0000250|UniProtKB:Q7RX33};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Protein required for the biosynthesis of ergothioneine (ERG), an unusual thio-histidine betaine amino acid that acts as an antioxidant against peroxide in conidia and contributes to conidial longevity (PubMed:27748436, PubMed:36142753). Also influences siderophore and gliotoxin biosynthesis (PubMed:27748436). Catalyzes the SAM-dependent triple methylation of the alpha-amino group of histidine to form hercynine and subsequent conjugation with cysteine and oxygen to form hercynylcysteine sulfoxide, the first two steps in the biosynthesis pathway of ergothioneine (By similarity). {ECO:0000250|UniProtKB:Q7RX33, ECO:0000269|PubMed:27748436, ECO:0000269|PubMed:36142753}.

Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Protein required for the biosynthesis of ergothioneine (ERG), an unusual thio-histidine betaine amino acid that acts as an antioxidant against peroxide in conidia and contributes to conidial longevity (By similarity). Catalyzes the SAM-dependent triple methylation of the alpha-amino group of histidine to form hercynine and subsequent conjugation with cysteine and oxygen to form hercynylcysteine sulfoxide, the first two steps in the biosynthesis pathway of ergothioneine (By similarity). Aspergillus oryzae produced only small amounts of ERG (PubMed:30286703). {ECO:0000250|UniProtKB:Q7RX33, ECO:0000269|PubMed:30286703}.
